pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh (line 16). File extension .flipchart is standard for ActivInspire presentations (confirmed by file-extensions.com and other sources).
Task description and setup_task.sh (line 12) which creates this directory. Standard Linux user document location.
Combination of expected_directory and expected_filename from task definition. Verified in task.json description line 6 and setup_task.sh.

Task description explicitly requires 3 pages: Page 1 (Introduction with title and stage names), Page 2 (Visual diagram with shapes), Page 3 (Quick assessment with questions). README.md section 'Goal' confirms this structure.

Task description: 'Water Cycle' is the Page 1 title; 'Evaporation', 'Condensation', 'Precipitation' are the three main stages confirmed by NOAA (https://www.noaa.gov/education/resource-collections/freshwater/water-cycle), NGSS standards (5-ESS2-1), and multiple educational sources; 'Quick Check' is the Page 3 title from task description.

Task description requires 'at least 3 shapes on the canvas to represent the three stages of the water cycle (e.g., a shape for the water body/evaporation stage, a shape for the cloud/condensation stage, and a shape for rain/precipitation)'. README.md 'Goal' section confirms this requirement.

Next Generation Science Standards (NGSS): 5-ESS2-1 requires 5th grade students to develop a model explaining how water cycles from Earth's surface to atmosphere and back, including evaporation, condensation, and precipitation (confirmed by nextgenscience.org and multiple educational standards documents). Water cycle is a core 5th grade earth science topic.

privileged_info_summary: This task requires creating a 3-page ActivInspire flipchart on the water cycle for 5th grade science, aligned with NGSS standard 5-ESS2-1. The three main water cycle stages are evaporation, condensation, and precipitation - all verified as core concepts in elementary water cycle curricula. The flipchart file format (.flipchart) is the standard format used by ActivInspire presentation software. The task requires specific content elements: Page 1 with 'Water Cycle' title and all three stage names, Page 2 with at least 3 shapes representing the stages with labels, and Page 3 with 'Quick Check' title and assessment questions. All metadata claims align with established science standards and task requirements.
